title: Ordered CatBoost binary specialist with rich categorical combinations
summary: Current best snippets are feature-engineering heavy but still mostly tabular-numeric in spirit.
rationale: Current best snippets are feature-engineering heavy but still mostly tabular-numeric in spirit. CatBoost’s ordered boosting and categorical combinations are designed to handle high-cardinality IDs and interaction-rich categorical structure without manual one-hot, which is a strong fit for `Driver`, `Race`, `Compound`, `Year`, and their combinations.
implementation_hint: Train a standalone CatBoostClassifier in binary mode with categorical columns left raw, enable combinations via depth and CTRs, and compare against the current model on strict grouped CV. Test minimal manual preprocessing; let CatBoost learn `Driver x Race x Compound` style interactions implicitly.
expected_effect: May capture identity-strategy interactions that hand-built heuristics miss, while remaining less leak-prone than ad hoc mean encodings.
risk: If validation is not strictly race-grouped, CatBoost can still look deceptively strong; GPU/seed variance may also be nontrivial.
